frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Yaar, aaj bhi office se late aaun toh maa ke haath ki simple dal roti yaad aa jaati hai, woh taste aur sukoon kisi cheez mein nahi. Sach bolun, ghar tabhi ghar lagta hai jab aurat bina hisaab kitab ke sabke liye pyaar se rasoi sambhale—meri wife bhi jab waise hi khayal rakhti hai na, dil khush ho jaata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes women’s domestic labor as love rather than work and frames cooking as what makes a home. It also sets the mother as the standard and implicitly expects the wife to reproduce the same unpaid care.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
